title: NCERT RIE Admission 2026: Registration Begins May 15, CEE Exam on June 28

detail.title: NCERT RIE Admission 2026: Registration Begins May 15, CEE Exam on June 28
detail.short_info: NCERT RIE admission 2026 registration starts May 15. Check CEE exam date, courses offered, eligibility, application process, and important dates for teacher education programs.

sections.heading: NCERT RIE Admission 2026: Registration Begins May 15, CEE Exam on June 28

content.text: NCERT RIE admission 2026 registration process will start from May 15, 2026, for various teacher education programmes offered through Regional Institutes of Education across India. The National Council of Educational Research and Training has released the official notification for admissions to undergraduate and postgraduate courses for the academic session 2026. Candidates who want to pursue a career in teaching can apply online through the Common Entrance Examination portal till June 5, 2026.

The CEE 2026 exam is gonna be conducted on June 28, 2026, at multiple centres throughout the country. This entrance test is mandatory for getting admission into B.Ed., M.Ed., integrated courses, and other teacher training programs at different RIE campuses.
Click Here: NCERT RIE Admission 2026 Notification
Here's the complete schedule for NCERT RIE admissions 2026 that every aspirant should know about:

rows: Last date to submit application form
Last date to upload qualifying exam marks for Integrated M.Sc.Ed. applicants
Last date to upload qualifying exam marks for B.Ed./M.Ed./B.Ed.-M.Ed./M.A. in Education Technology applicants

content.text: Candidates should note that the application window is open for approximately three weeks, so don't wait till last moment to apply.

NCERT is offering multiple teacher education programmes through RIE admissions 2026 for different qualification levels. The courses available are designed to prepare quality teachers for secondary and higher secondary schools.

items: Integrated M.Sc.Ed. (6-Year Programme) - For students who completed 12th with PCM and want to become higher secondary teachers in Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics
Bachelor of Education - B.Ed. (2-Year Programme) - For graduates wanting to teach at secondary level in Science & Mathematics or Language & Social Science
Integrated B.Ed.-M.Ed. (3-Year Programme) - A combined degree program for B.Ed. holders
Master of Education - M.Ed. (2-Year Programme) - Primarily designed for preparing teacher educators and education professionals
Master of Arts in Education Technology (2-Year Programme) - Specialized course in educational technology applications

content.text: These courses are available at various RIE campuses located in Ajmer, Bhopal, Bhubaneswar, Mysore, and Shillong.
The eligibility requirements vary depending on which course you're applying for in NCERT RIE admission 2026:
For Integrated M.Sc.Ed. (6-Year):

items: 12th pass in Science with PCM stream
Minimum 50% marks in aggregate (45% for SC/ST/PwD candidates)

content.text: For B.Ed. (2-Year):

items: Bachelor's degree in Science/Arts/Commerce/Social Science/Humanities
At least 50% marks in graduation or post-graduation (45% for reserved categories)

content.text: For Integrated B.Ed.-M.Ed. (3-Year):

items: B.Ed. degree with minimum 50% marks
Relaxation of 5% for SC/ST/PwD candidates

content.text: For M.Ed. (2-Year):

items: B.Ed./B.A.B.Ed./B.Sc.Ed./B.El.Ed. or equivalent teaching degree
Minimum 50% marks (45% for SC/ST/PwD)

content.text: For M.A. in Education Technology:

items: Bachelor's degree with relevant background
Minimum qualifying percentage as per NCERT norms

content.text: Candidates appearing in their qualifying examination can also apply, but they must upload their final marks by the specified deadline.

sections.heading: How to Apply for NCERT RIE Admission 2026

content.text: The application process for RIE CEE 2026 is completely online and pretty straightforward. Follow these steps to complete your registration:

items: Visit the official CEE portal at cee.ncert.gov.in
Click on the "NCERT RIE Admission 2026" or "Online Application" link (will be active from May 15)
Register yourself by providing basic details like name, email ID, and mobile number
Fill the application form with personal, educational, and other required information
Upload scanned copies of photograph, signature, and necessary documents
Select your preferred course and RIE centre
Pay the application fee through online mode (credit card/debit card/net banking/UPI)
Submit the form and take a printout for future reference

content.text: Make sure all the details you enter are accurate because mistakes can lead to rejection of application. Keep your valid email ID and phone number handy as all communication will be sent through these.
The Common Entrance Examination for RIE admission 2026 follows a specific pattern that candidates should understand before appearing:

items: Exam Mode: Offline (pen and paper based)
Exam Date: June 28, 2026 (Sunday)
Duration: Specific timing will be mentioned on admit card
Question Type: Multiple choice questions (MCQs)
Subjects Covered: General Knowledge, Teaching Aptitude, Reasoning, English Language, and subject-specific questions depending on course

content.text: The syllabus includes topics from graduation level for most courses, while integrated programmes may have questions based on 12th standard subjects. Detailed syllabus and exam pattern will be available in the information brochure on the official website.

sections.heading: Admit Card and Answer Key Details

content.text: NCERT will release the RIE CEE 2026 admit card on June 20, 2026, through the official portal. Candidates need to login with their registration credentials to download the hall ticket.
The admit card will contain important details like:

items: Candidate's name and photograph
Roll number and registration number
Exam centre name and address
Exam date and reporting time
Important instructions for the exam day

content.text: Answer Key Release: The provisional answer key for CEE 2026 will be published on the official website after the examination is over. Candidates can raise objections against the answer key within the specified time frame, and NCERT will consider valid objections before declaring final results. Students are advised to keep checking the official website regularly for updates regarding answer key, results, and counselling schedule.

seo_tags: NCERT Regional Institute of Education Admission 2026
